questions.question: What is the full format for identifying a Docker image?
What does the 'latest' tag mean in Docker?
options: It always points to the most recently built image
It is the default tag, but not always the newest
It refers to the last stable release
It is automatically updated every day
questions.answer: It is the default tag, but not always the newest

questions.question: Which Python image variant is the smallest?
Approximately how large is the full python:3.12 image?
What does the '-slim' suffix indicate in a Docker image tag?
options: The image is a minimal variant without extras
The image has no operating system
questions.answer: The image is a minimal variant without extras

questions.question: What base operating system does the 'alpine' tag variant use?
What does the 'bookworm' tag refer to in Docker image tags?
What are Docker image layers?
options: Separate containers stacked on top of each other
Each instruction in a Dockerfile creates a layer that is cached and shared
Virtual network interfaces inside the container
Different versions of the same image
questions.answer: Each instruction in a Dockerfile creates a layer that is cached and shared

questions.question: Which command pulls an image from Docker Hub?
Which command lists all locally available Docker images and their sizes?
What does the 'docker inspect' command do?
options: Shows detailed metadata about an image or container
Scans an image for security vulnerabilities
questions.answer: Shows detailed metadata about an image or container

questions.question: Which command runs a container interactively with a terminal?
What does the '-d' flag do when running a container?
options: Deletes the container after it stops
Runs the container in detached (background) mode
questions.answer: Runs the container in detached (background) mode

questions.question: What flag is used to assign a custom name to a container?
Which command shows only the currently running containers?
What does 'docker ps -a' show that 'docker ps' does not?
Which command displays the logs of a running container named 'web-server'?
What does the 'docker stats' command display?
options: The number of images on disk
Real-time CPU, memory, and network usage for running containers
A history of all pulled images
questions.answer: Real-time CPU, memory, and network usage for running containers

questions.question: Which flag passes an environment variable into a container?
What does the '--rm' flag do when running a container?
options: Removes the container image after use
Automatically deletes the container when it exits
Runs the container in read-only mode
questions.answer: Automatically deletes the container when it exits

questions.question: What does the '-w' flag set when running a container?
options: The maximum wait time before timeout

questions.question: Which command stops a running container named 'api-server'?
Which command removes a stopped container named 'web-server'?
What is a key superpower of Docker demonstrated by running multiple Python versions simultaneously?
options: It compiles code faster than native installations
It runs different versions side by side with no conflicts and nothing installed on the host
It automatically merges code from different Python versions
It converts Python code to machine code
questions.answer: It runs different versions side by side with no conflicts and nothing installed on the host

questions.question: What does the '--format' flag do with 'docker inspect'?
options: Changes the output file format to CSV
Applies a Go template to extract specific fields from the output
Sets the image format to a compressed archive
questions.answer: Applies a Go template to extract specific fields from the output
